Home / Video Conferencing / Alternatives to Google Meet

2 open source alternatives to Google Meet

Meetings embedded in Google Workspace. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Google Meet

  • Tied to Google account ecosystem.
  • Free tier has 60-minute limit on group calls.
  • Privacy concerns for non-Workspace users.

Current Google Meet pricing (for reference): Free (time-limited for groups); full access with Workspace.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Jitsi Meet
Open source video meetings you can self-host or use free. 		Instant browser meetings with no Google account. Apache-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
BigBlueButton
Open source web conferencing built for online learning. 		Structured learning and webinar workflows. LGPL-3.0 ★★★★☆ Yes

1. Jitsi Meet — Instant browser meetings with no Google account.

Open source video meetings you can self-host or use free.

Strengths

  • Free public instance (meet.jit.si) with no sign-up.
  • Self-hostable for privacy.
  • Webhook and SDK integrations available.

Weaknesses

  • Tuning a self-hosted instance for scale takes ops work.
  • Advanced features (e2ee, breakouts) evolving.
  • No native desktop app.
License: Apache-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud optionMobile: iOS, Android

Jitsi Meet homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Meet vs Jitsi Meet →

2. BigBlueButton — Structured learning and webinar workflows.

Open source web conferencing built for online learning.

Strengths

  • Designed for virtual classrooms with whiteboard and polls.
  • Recording, breakout rooms, and captions built in.
  • Integrates with Moodle and other LMS.

Weaknesses

  • Demanding hardware for self-hosting.
  • Setup and maintenance are not beginner-friendly.
  • UI feels academic, not corporate.
License: LGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 4/5 Hosted cloud option

BigBlueButton homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Meet vs BigBlueButton →
